images.caption: Figure 2:Illustrative example of 1D denoising process mapping Gaussian noise to a tri-modal distribution, withQQdefined as negative L2 distance to the optimal actiona∗a^{*}. We compare the base BC flow and three critic-gradient guidance methods (BPTT, OOD, QGF) across three guidance weights. While BPTT and QGF converge toa∗a^{*},guidance with the OOD gradient∇atQ​(s,at)\nabla_{a_{t}}Q(s,a_{t})does not result in the optimal solution. Further,Fig.˜14showsBPTT can be highly unstable.

Figure 3:Sensitivity of different gradient estimators to noise in the action space: for each gradient estimatorGG, the plot shows the cosine similarity betweenG​(s,at)G(s,a_{t})andG​(s,at+ϵ)G(s,a_{t}+\epsilon). Our proposed gradient estimator has the least variance and least sensitivity to noise. Averaged over2020tasks and44seeds.

Figure 4:QQ-values of the denoised action with different gradient guidance, which roughly correlate with performance. Averaged over2020OGBench environments.QGF is the best gradient-based optimizer, getting close to the performance of the best-of-n oracle.

Figure 5:Offline RL performance at500500k training steps (20 tasks, 10 seeds): QGF beats all previous test-time methods and is competitive with the best training-time method. See task breakdown inFig.˜11.
Figure 6:Compute requirements for different test-time methods: BFN needs orders of magnitude more FLOPs compared to QGF and GradStep.
Figure 6:Compute requirements for different test-time methods: BFN needs orders of magnitude more FLOPs compared to QGF and GradStep.

Figure 7:Offline RL performance at 500k training steps (20 tasks, 10 seeds) evaluated with best-of-N sampling. QGF outperforms BFN (N=4), and QGF+BFN matches BFN with much higher test-time compute budget. See task breakdown inFig.˜12.

Figure 8:Offline goal-conditioned RL performanceat11M training steps (25 tasks, 10 seeds): While QGF underperforms the best baseline on the simplest task, it is consistently the best performing method for the harder tasks, showing that the gradient estimator scales well to long horizon tasks. See task breakdown inFig.˜13.

Figure 8:Offline goal-conditioned RL performanceat11M training steps (25 tasks, 10 seeds): While QGF underperforms the best baseline on the simplest task, it is consistently the best performing method for the harder tasks, showing that the gradient estimator scales well to long horizon tasks. See task breakdown inFig.˜13.
Figure 9:Scaling model size of QGF: compared to training-time methods such as QAM[33], QGF benefits much more from increasing the model size. Averaged over 5 tasks inc3.
Figure 10:QGF can work with different types of critics and performs better when critics are better (20 tasks, 4 seeds). See task break down inFig.˜19.
Figure 11:Offline RL performance at 500k training steps per environment (1010seeds each).
Figure 12:Offline RL with Best-of-N sampling at 500k training steps per environment (1010seeds each).
Figure 13:Offline goal-conditioned RL at 1M training steps (10 seeds each). See the domain-specific hyperparameters inTable˜4.
